nodes.label: Cross-Platform Human Movement Detection Master Plan
nodes.norm_label: cross-platform human movement detection master plan

nodes.label: Hop Detection State Machine (STANDING/RISING/AIRBORNE/LANDING)
nodes.rationale: Modeled as a phased state machine (rising/airborne/landing) rather than a single threshold check so that walking and crouching can be explicitly rejected and exactly one event is emitted per valid hop.

nodes.label: Turn-Around Detection State Sequence (FRONT/SIDE/BACK/SIDE/FRONT)
nodes.rationale: Uses accumulated body rotation through front/side/back/side/front classification, with tolerance for temporary landmark loss, so half-turns and side-and-return motions are rejected while full 360-degree turns in either direction are accepted.

nodes.label: Agent Responsibilities (per-phase plan/approve/implement/report loop)
nodes.rationale: The agent must present a plan and wait for explicit approval before touching files, then stop after each phase, so that scope stays bounded, tests are never skipped, and the human retains control over architectural changes rather than the agent silently continuing to the next phase.

nodes.label: Development Principles (Section 3)
nodes.rationale: Principles such as keeping platform-specific code out of the shared module, using timestamps instead of assuming a fixed frame rate, avoiding global mutable state, and keeping camera/pose/detection/UI separated exist to keep movement detection deterministic, reproducible, and testable without camera hardware.

nodes.label: Phase 1 Plan — Base KMP Project Structure (Android-only)
nodes.norm_label: phase 1 plan — base kmp project structure (android-only)

nodes.label: iOS Scope Deferred to Milestone 4
nodes.rationale: No macOS/Xcode machine exists yet, and the roadmap already flags this as a hard blocker for Milestone 4. Phase 1 was scoped to only stand up shared (Android/JVM targets) plus androidApp, so nothing gets built 'blind' and unverified; the iOS target is added once a Mac is available.

nodes.label: Toolchain Setup via Android Studio (not bare CLI toolchain)
nodes.rationale: Android Studio bundles JDK + Android SDK + emulator manager in one installation, resolving all missing prerequisites (JDK, Android SDK, Gradle support) together rather than assembling a bare command-line toolchain manually.
nodes.norm_label: toolchain setup via android studio (not bare cli toolchain)

nodes.label: Migration to com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library (AGP 9)
nodes.rationale: The master plan's original Phase 1 design (androidTarget() inside the KMP block combined with the classic com.android.library plugin) is incompatible with AGP 9.0+, bundled by this machine's Android Studio. Resolved by switching to the new AGP9-mandatory com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library plugin and its kotlin { android { ... } } DSL; the plugin's own androidLibrary { ... } block was further found deprecated in favor of android { ... } in AGP 9.3.0.

nodes.label: Gradle Daemon Heap Size Fix (OutOfMemoryError)
nodes.rationale: The default Gradle daemon heap (512MB/384MB metaspace) was too small for lintVitalAnalyzeRelease's UAST analysis and crashed with an OutOfMemoryError. Fixed by adding gradle.properties with org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx2048m -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=1024m.
